name: analyze_websocket_messages
description: Analyzes WebSocket messages and groups them by pattern/fingerprint. Essential for understanding binary/protobuf message types in live streaming scenarios. Returns statistics and sample indices for each message type.

wsid.description: The wsid of the WebSocket connection to analyze.

direction.description: Only analyze messages in this direction.

name: break_on_xhr
description: Sets a breakpoint that triggers when an XHR/Fetch request URL contains the specified string.

url.description: URL pattern to break on (partial match).

name: evaluate_script
description:
Evaluate a JavaScript function inside the currently selected page. Returns the response as JSON
so returned values have to JSON-serializable. When execution is paused at a breakpoint, automatically evaluates in the paused call frame context.

mainWorld.description: Execute the function in the page main world instead of the default isolated context. Use this when you need to access page-defined globals (e.g. window.bdms, window.app). The function must be synchronous and return a JSON-serializable value.

name: get_console_message
description: Gets a console message by its ID. You can get all messages by calling list_console_messages.

msgid.description: The msgid of a console message on the page from the listed console messages

name: get_network_request
description: Gets a network request by an optional reqid, if omitted returns the currently selected request in the DevTools Network panel.

reqid.description: The reqid of the network request. If omitted returns the currently selected request in the DevTools Network panel.

name: get_paused_info
description: Gets information about the current paused state including call stack, current location, and scope variables. Use this after a breakpoint is hit to understand the execution context.

includeScopes.description: Whether to include scope variables (default: true).

name: get_request_initiator
description: Gets the JavaScript call stack that initiated a network request. This helps trace which code triggered an API call.

requestId.description: The request ID (from list_network_requests) to get the initiator for.

name: get_script_source
description: Gets a small snippet of a JavaScript script source by URL (recommended) or script ID. Supports line range (for normal files) or character offset (for minified single-line files). Prefer using url over scriptId — URLs remain stable across page navigations while script IDs become invalid after reload. IMPORTANT: This tool is designed for reading small code regions (e.g. around breakpoints or search results). You MUST always specify startLine/endLine or offset/length. To read an entire script file, use curl to download it by its URL instead.

url.description: Script URL (preferred). Stable across page navigations. Exact match first, then substring match.

scriptId.description: Script ID (from list_scripts). Becomes invalid after page navigation — prefer url instead.

startLine.description: Start line number (1-based). Use for multi-line files.

endLine.description: End line number (1-based). Use for multi-line files.

offset.description: Character offset to start from (0-based). Use for minified single-line files.

length.description: Number of characters to return when using offset (default: 1000).

name: get_websocket_message
description: Gets a single WebSocket message by its frame index. Use get_websocket_messages or analyze_websocket_messages first to find the frame index.

wsid.description: The wsid of the WebSocket connection.

name: get_websocket_messages
description: Gets messages for a WebSocket connection. IMPORTANT: For binary/protobuf messages (like live streaming), use analyze_websocket_messages FIRST to understand message types, then use groupId parameter to filter specific types. Default mode shows summary only.

wsid.description: The wsid of the WebSocket connection.

groupId.description: Filter by group ID (A, B, C, ...). Get group IDs from analyze_websocket_messages first.

show_content.description: Set to true to show full message payload. Default false (summary only) to avoid large binary output.

name: inject_before_load
description: Injects a JavaScript script that runs before any page script on every page load (including refreshes and navigations). Persists until removed. Uses CDP Page.addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument.

script.description: JavaScript code to inject. Runs before any page script. Example: Object.defineProperty(window, "h5sign", { set(v) { debugger; this._h5sign = v; }, get() { return this._h5sign; } })

name: list_breakpoints
description: Lists all active breakpoints in the current debugging session. Breakpoints persist across page navigations and are automatically restored after reload/goto/back/forward.

name: list_console_messages
description: List all console messages for the currently selected page since the last navigation.

pageSize.description: Maximum number of messages to return. When omitted, returns all requests.

pageIdx.description: Page number to return (0-based). When omitted, returns the first page.

types.description: Filter messages to only return messages of the specified resource types. When omitted or empty, returns all messages.

includePreservedMessages.description: Set to true to return the preserved messages over the last 3 navigations.

name: list_frames
description: Lists all frames (including iframes) in the current page as a tree. Shows frame index, name, and URL. Use select_frame to switch execution context to a specific frame.

name: list_network_requests
description: List network requests for the currently selected page since the last navigation. Results are sorted newest-first. By default returns the 20 most recent requests; use pageSize/pageIdx to paginate.

pageSize.description: Maximum number of requests to return. Defaults to 20.

pageIdx.description: Page number to return (0-based). When omitted, returns the first page.

resourceTypes.description: Filter requests to only return requests of the specified resource types. When omitted or empty, returns all requests.

includePreservedRequests.description: Set to true to return the preserved requests over the last 3 navigations.

name: list_pages
description: Get a list of pages open in the browser.

name: list_scripts
description: Lists all JavaScript scripts loaded in the current page. Returns script ID, URL, and source map information. Use this to find scripts before setting breakpoints or searching. Script IDs are automatically refreshed after page navigation, so listed IDs are always valid.

filter.description: Optional filter string to match against script URLs (case-insensitive partial match).

name: list_websocket_connections
description: List all WebSocket connections. After getting wsid, use analyze_websocket_messages(wsid) FIRST to understand message patterns before viewing individual messages.

pageSize.description: Maximum number of connections to return. When omitted, returns all connections.

pageIdx.description: Page number to return (0-based). When omitted, returns the first page.

urlFilter.description: Filter connections by URL. Only connections containing this substring will be returned.

includePreservedConnections.description: Set to true to return the preserved connections over the last 3 navigations.

name: navigate_page
description: Navigates the currently selected page to a URL, or performs back/forward/reload navigation. Waits for DOMContentLoaded event (not full page load). Default timeout is 10 seconds. After navigation, stale script IDs are cleared and fresh ones are captured automatically. All breakpoints (URL, XHR, DOM) are preserved across navigation.

type.description: Navigate the page by URL, back or forward in history, or reload.

ignoreCache.description: Whether to ignore cache on reload.

timeout.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds. If set to 0, the default timeout will be used.

name: new_page
description: Creates a new page and navigates to the specified URL. Waits for DOMContentLoaded event (not full page load). Default timeout is 10 seconds.

url.description: URL to load in a new page.

timeout.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds. If set to 0, the default timeout will be used.

name: pause
description: Pauses JavaScript execution at the current point. Use this to interrupt running code.

name: remove_all_breakpoints
description: Removes all active breakpoints. Use this to clean up debugging state.

name: remove_breakpoint
description: Removes a breakpoint by its ID. Use list_breakpoints to see active breakpoints.

breakpointId.description: The breakpoint ID to remove (from list_breakpoints or set_breakpoint_on_text).

name: remove_injected_script
description: Removes a script previously injected with inject_before_load.

name: remove_xhr_breakpoint
description: Removes an XHR/Fetch breakpoint.

url.description: The URL pattern to remove breakpoint for.

name: resume
description: Resumes JavaScript execution after being paused at a breakpoint. Execution continues until the next breakpoint or completion.

name: search_in_sources
description: Searches for a string or regex pattern in all loaded JavaScript sources. Returns matching lines with script ID, URL, and line number. Use get_script_source with startLine/endLine to view full context around matches.

query.description: The search query (string or regex pattern).

caseSensitive.description: Whether the search should be case-sensitive.

isRegex.description: Whether to treat the query as a regular expression.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 30).

maxLineLength.description: Maximum characters per matched line preview (default: 150). Increase if you need more context around the match.

excludeMinified.description: Skip minified files (files with very long lines). Default: true.

urlFilter.description: Only search scripts whose URL contains this string (case-insensitive).

name: select_frame
description: Selects a frame (by index from list_frames) as the execution context for evaluate_script, hook_function, inspect_object, and other tools that run JavaScript in the page.

frameIdx.description: The frame index (from list_frames). 0 = main frame.

name: select_page
description: Select a page as a context for future tool calls.

pageIdx.description: The index of the page to select. Call list_pages to list pages.

name: set_breakpoint_on_text
description: Sets a breakpoint on specific code (function name, statement, etc.) by searching for it and automatically determining the exact position. Works with both normal and minified files. Breakpoints persist across page navigations.

text.description: The code text to find and set breakpoint on (e.g., "function myFunc", "fetchData(", "apiCall").

urlFilter.description: Only search in scripts whose URL contains this string (case-insensitive).

occurrence.description: Which occurrence to break on (1 = first, 2 = second, etc.).

condition.description: Optional condition expression. Breakpoint only triggers when this evaluates to true.

name: step_into
description: Steps into the next function call. Use this to enter and debug function bodies.

name: step_out
description: Steps out of the current function, continuing until the function returns. Use this to quickly exit a function.

name: step_over
description: Steps over to the next statement, treating function calls as a single step. Use this to move through code without entering function bodies.

name: take_screenshot
description: Take a screenshot of the page or element.

format.description: Type of format to save the screenshot as. Default is "png"

quality.description: Compression quality for JPEG format (0-100). Higher values mean better quality but larger file sizes. Ignored for PNG format.

fullPage.description: If set to true takes a screenshot of the full page instead of the currently visible viewport. Incompatible with uid.

filePath.description: The absolute path, or a path relative to the current working directory, to save the screenshot to instead of attaching it to the response.

name: trace_function
description: Traces calls to a function by its name in the source code. Works for ANY function including module-internal functions (webpack/rollup bundled). Uses "logpoints" (conditional breakpoints) to log arguments without pausing execution. Trace breakpoints persist across page navigations.

functionName.description: The function name to trace. Will search for "function NAME" or "NAME = function" or "NAME(" patterns.

urlFilter.description: Only search in scripts matching this URL pattern.

logArgs.description: Whether to log function arguments (default: true).

pause.description: Whether to actually pause execution (default: false, just logs).

traceId.description: Custom ID for this trace. Used to identify in logs.
